description: Failure envelope: at least one reproducible, cause-labelled failure regime
summary: D1 v0.1.1 identifies a convergence_failure regime in the sigma_x thermal fixture. All 20 frozen coupling_strength sweep points in 0.05 to 1.0 classified as convergence_failure under the parity-aware r_4 metric. The Path B route verifies stability under the operational omega_c perturbations and records the runner-threaded upper_cutoff_factor perturbations as a Path-B-specific limitation because exact_finite_env does not consume that knob.
limitations: The verdict uses benchmark-side Path B numerical L_4 extraction and carries the documented finite-env floor; analytic Path A remains pending.

upper_cutoff_factor perturbations are not operational in the current exact_finite_env Path B extraction, although they are threaded by the runner; omega_c perturbations are operational model-spec mutations.
alpha_crit is not bracketed inside the frozen sweep range because the first swept point already fails; the boundary is below coupling_strength=0.05 under this run.
This PASS authorises a cause-labelled failure-envelope report, not convergence reliability in arbitrary strong-coupling regimes.
